Brazil, Guyana, Venezuela Fuel South America’s Oil Export Boom
headlines.summary: South America has raised its oil exports more than the U.S. has done so far this year as key producers in the region boosted production and shipments to a world scrambling for crude that’s not dependent on the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: A New U.S. Battery Line Designed to Make Energy Storage Safer and Cheaper
headlines.summary: The US has recently launched a new battery production line, which is expected to help researchers develop safer and cheaper energy storage technologies for the electric grid. The new line is housed at the Grid Storage Launchpad (GSL), a 93,000-square-foot research facility. It is

headlines.title: Iran's Oil Exports Collapse to Six-Year Low as Blockade Tightens
headlines.summary: Iran's oil exports fell to their lowest level in at least six years in May as the U.S. naval blockade continued to choke off crude shipments and leave tens of millions of barrels stranded at sea. According to shipping data from Vortexa, Iran exported just 209,000 barrels per day

headlines.title: U.S. Oil Shocks Don't Hit Like They Used To, Fed Study Finds
headlines.summary: The United States still feels oil shocks. It just doesn't feel them the way it did when America was dancing to disco and waiting in gas lines. If the Fed is right, the idea that every oil shock leads to recession is outdated. A new study from the Federal Reserve Bank of Boston fi

headlines.title: Dangote Breaks Ground on 700,000-Bpd Second Crude Processing Unit
headlines.summary: Africa's biggest refinery is getting bigger. headlines.title: Russia Admits Oil Output Is Falling as Ukrainian Drone Strikes Hits Refineries
